divorce.explanation: Divorce is the legal process of ending a marriage through court.
procedure: File a divorce petition in the family court.
Court sends notice to the opposite party.
Both parties appear before the court.
Mediation or counselling may be conducted.
If reconciliation fails, the case proceeds.
Court issues divorce decree after hearing both sides.
evidence: Proof supporting the reason for divorce
opposite_party_action: May file claims regarding maintenance or custody
divorce.punishment_or_outcome: This is generally a civil/family matter. The main outcome is divorce decree, maintenance, custody, or property-related orders.
risk_factors: Delay in filing may complicate documentation
Maintenance and property disputes may arise
divorce.next_step: Consult a family lawyer and file the petition in the appropriate family court.

cheating.explanation: Cheating happens when a person dishonestly deceives another person and causes wrongful loss.
law: IPC Section 420 - Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
IPC Section 406 - Criminal breach of trust
procedure: Collect proof of the transaction or promise.
File a police complaint or FIR if criminal cheating is involved.
Submit bank records, chats, receipts, or agreements.
Police may investigate and call both parties.
If sufficient evidence exists, further legal action may follow.
opposite_party_action: May claim it was a civil dispute
May produce a different version of agreement
cheating.punishment_or_outcome: Under IPC 420, punishment may extend up to 7 years and fine.
risk_factors: Lack of written proof weakens the case
Delay in complaint may affect investigation
Oral promises are harder to prove
cheating.next_step: Preserve all transaction and communication records and file a complaint quickly.

defamation.explanation: Defamation means making or publishing false statements that harm a person's reputation.
procedure: Collect proof of defamatory statement.
Preserve screenshots, audio, video, or publication copies.
Consult a lawyer for criminal complaint or civil remedy.
File complaint before the appropriate court if required.
evidence: Proof of reputational harm if available
opposite_party_action: May claim fair comment or opinion
May deny intention to harm reputation
defamation.punishment_or_outcome: Under IPC 500, punishment may extend to 2 years, or fine, or both.
risk_factors: Deleted online posts should be preserved quickly
Truth and public interest may be used as defence
Delay may make evidence collection difficult
defamation.next_step: Save the content immediately and seek legal advice before filing complaint.

domestic violence.explanation: Domestic violence includes physical, emotional, verbal, sexual, or economic abuse within a domestic relationship.
law: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005
IPC Section 498A - Cruelty by husband or relatives
procedure: Approach police, protection officer, or magistrate.
File complaint with supporting facts.
Seek protection order, residence order, or maintenance if needed.
Medical treatment and documentation should be obtained in injury cases.
evidence: Financial records if economic abuse is involved
domestic violence.punishment_or_outcome: Depending on facts, relief may include protection orders, maintenance, residence orders, and criminal action under relevant provisions.
risk_factors: Delay in medical proof may weaken injury evidence
Dependence on opposite party may affect complaint process
domestic violence.next_step: Ensure immediate safety first, preserve evidence, and approach police or legal support.

theft.explanation: Theft means dishonestly taking movable property without consent.
procedure: File a complaint or FIR at the nearest police station.
Provide item details and place/time of theft.
Submit any CCTV footage or ownership proof.
Police may investigate and recover stolen property.
evidence: IMEI or serial number if device stolen
opposite_party_action: May hide or transfer stolen property
theft.punishment_or_outcome: Punishment under IPC 379 may extend up to 3 years, or fine, or both.
risk_factors: Missing ownership proof weakens recovery claim
Delay in reporting may reduce recovery chances
theft.next_step: Report immediately and keep all ownership proof ready.
